query: Create a comprehensive security audit checklist for a FinTech app handling transactions up to $1M — cover OWASP, PCI-DSS, fraud detection, and regulatory requirements

single.output:
# Comprehensive Security Audit Checklist for FinTech Applications

## Scope: Transactions up to $1M | Scope: Application, Infrastructure, and Compliance

---

# TABLE OF CONTENTS

1. [OWASP Top 10 Application Security](#1-owasp-top-10)
2. [PCI-DSS Requirements](#2-pci-dss)
3. [Authentication & Authorization](#3-auth--auth)
4. [Transaction Security Controls](#4-transaction-security)
5. [Fraud Detection & Prevention](#5-fraud-detection)
6. [Data Protection & Privacy](#6-data-protection)
7. [Infrastructure & Network Security](#7-infrastructure)
8. [API Security](#8-api-security)
9. [Incident Response & Monitoring](#9-monitoring-incident-response)
10. [Regulatory Compliance](#10-regulatory)
11. [Vendor & Third-Party Risk](#11-vendor-risk)
12. [Executive Summary Template](#12-executive-summary)

---

# 1. OWASP Top 10 Application Security

## 1.1 Broken Access Control

| Checkpoint | Requirement | Evidence | Status |
|------------|-------------|----------|--------|
| AC-001 | Role-based access control (RBAC) implemented for all endpoints | Policy docs + code review | ☐ |
| AC-002 | Principle of least privilege enforced for all user roles | Access matrix review | ☐ |
| AC-003 | Direct object reference prevention (IDOR protection) | Penetration test results | ☐ |
| AC-004 | Server-side authorization enforced (not client-side) | Code review audit | ☐ |
| AC-005 | CORS policy correctly configured | Configuration review | ☐ |
| AC-006 | Access control matrix documented and reviewed quarterly | Audit logs + review docs | ☐ |
| AC-007 | No privilege escalation possible via API manipulation | AuthZ test results | ☐ |
| AC-008 | Rate limiting enforced per user/role/endpoint | Load test documentation | ☐ |

## 1.2 Cryptographic Failures

| Checkpoint | Requirement | Evidence | Status |
|------------|-------------|----------|--------|
| CF-001 | TLS 1.2+ enforced for all data in transit | SSL scan results | ☐ |
| CF-002 | AES-256 encryption for data at rest | Key management audit |

fixed.output:
# Comprehensive Security Audit Checklist
## FinTech Application — Transactions Up to $1M
### Version 2.0 — Enhanced & Gaps Addressed

---

## 1. PRE-AUDIT PREPARATION & SCOPE DEFINITION

### 1.1 Documentation Collection

| Item | Description | Priority | Specific Requirements |
|------|-------------|----------|----------------------|
| Architecture Diagrams | System topology, data flow, third-party integrations | Critical | Must include: network zones, DMZ topology, cloud VPC peering, data flow with direction arrows, trust boundaries, integration touchpoints |
| Asset Inventory | All hardware, software, cloud resources, APIs | Critical | Must enumerate: asset tags, ownership, data classification, EOL dates, licensing, vulnerability scan dates |
| User Roles & Permissions Matrix | Role definitions, access controls, segregation of duties | Critical | Must define: role hierarchies, SoD rules, MFA requirements per role, privileged access count, access review frequency |
| Data Flow Diagrams | PII, financial data, cardholder data paths | Critical | Must map: GDPR Article 30 data categories, PCI-DSS cardholder data flows, cross-border transfer paths, retention periods per data type |
| Previous Audit Reports | Historical findings, remediation status | High | Must include: remediation evidence, remediation timeline adherence, recurring findings flagged as Critical |
| Incident Response Plan | Detection, containment, recovery procedures | Critical | **See Section 4.1 for quantified SLA targets** |
| Business Continuity Plan | DR/BCP documentation | High | Must include: RTO ≤ 4 hours for core systems, RPO ≤ 1 hour for transaction data, failover procedures, communication plan |
| Third-Party Vendor Contracts | SLA, security obligations, liability clauses | High | Must address: GDPR Article 28 processor agreements, sub-processor notification, breach notification ≤ 24 hours, audit rights, liability caps |
| Encryption Key Management | Key rotation, storage, access procedures |

gnn.output:
# Comprehensive Security Audit Checklist Report: High-Value Transaction FinTech Application

## Executive Overview

This unified report synthesizes technical and business security perspectives to establish a comprehensive audit framework for FinTech applications processing transactions up to **$1 million per transaction**. The analysis addresses the original query requirements across four critical domains: OWASP compliance standards, PCI-DSS v4.0 requirements, fraud detection mechanisms, and multi-jurisdictional regulatory obligations.

**Critical Date Reminder**: The PCI-DSS v4.0 full compliance deadline is **March 31, 2025** (TECHNICAL perspective). Organizations still operating under v3.2.1 must prioritize immediate migration to avoid regulatory penalties.

**Financial Risk Context**: The average cost of a data breach in financial services reached **$5.9 million in 2023**, according to IBM/Ponemon research (BUSINESS perspective). Combined with GDPR penalties reaching **€20 million or 4% of global annual turnover**—whichever is higher—business exposure from security failures is substantial.

---

## 1. Scope and Methodology

### 1.1 Audit Scope Definition

The security audit framework encompasses:

- **Application Layer Security**: Mobile application (iOS/Android) and web application controls
- **Infrastructure Controls**: Network communication, server-side encryption, and access management
- **Fraud Prevention Mechanisms**: Transaction monitoring, anomaly detection, and identity verification
- **Compliance Obligations**: PCI-DSS v4.0, GDPR, OWASP standards, and transaction-specific requirements

### 1.2 Verification Levels

Per OWASP Mobile Application Security Verification Standard **version 2.1** (released Q4 2023), applications handling high-value transactions should target **MASVS Level 2 (L2)** or higher verification. The L2 designation provides defense-in-depth protections essential for financial exposure at the $1M transaction level (TECHNICAL perspective)

cascade.output:
# FINAL QUALITY REVIEW: Gap Closure Verification

## GAP CLOSURE ASSESSMENT

| Gap | Status | Evidence |
|-----|--------|----------|
| **GAP 1** — Data Flow Diagram specificity | ✅ **FULLY ADDRESSED** | Revised section includes: DFD Level 1-2 (Yourdon/DeMarco), transaction threshold ≥$1,000 or ≥500 records/day, quarterly internal review, 72-hour update triggers, annual penetration-test validation, cloud/on-prem/third-party API scope, and designated Data Custodian ownership |
| **GAP 2** — Third-party questionnaire standardization | ⚠️ **PARTIALLY ADDRESSED** | Sub-item table for item 6 is initiated but content is truncated; does not include: questionnaire standard name (CAIQ/SIG), ≥70% scoring threshold, right-to-audit clause requirement, or annual VRM review cadence |
| **GAP 3** — PCI-DSS merchant level determination | ❌ **NOT ADDRESSED** | Section 1.2 compliance table remains identical to original; no merchant/service provider level, SAQ vs. ROC scope, or cardholder data flow segmentation documented |

---

## CLEAN FINAL VERSION — REVISED SECURITY AUDIT CHECKLIST

---

# Comprehensive Security Audit Checklist — FinTech Application ($1M Transactions)

---

## 1. GOVERNANCE & RISK MANAGEMENT

### 1.1 Security Governance Framework

| Item | Control | Reference | Priority |
|------|---------|-----------|----------|
| ☐ | Establish Security Charter approved by Board | ISO 27001 Clause 5.2 | Critical |
| ☐ | Define Security Governance Structure (CISO, SecurityOps, DevSecOps) | — | Critical |
| ☐ | Document Data Classification Policy (Public, Internal, Confidential, Restricted) | — | High |
| ☐ | Conduct Annual Enterprise Risk Assessment | SOX, ISO 27001 | Critical |
| ☐ | **REVISED:** Maintain Data Flow Diagram for all PII/PHI/Financial Data | — | Critical |

> **Revision Justification:** For a $1M transaction-volume system, the DFD must capture every data custodian and transit point above a defined threshold to identify custody gaps.

| Sub-item | Specification |
|--

ensemble.output:
# UNIFIED SECURITY AUDIT CHECKLIST: FinTech Application (Transactions up to $1M)

---

## Executive Overview

Best source: **Report B (GNN-5-perspective)**
Content: This report provides the essential regulatory context that frames the entire audit. It establishes the market context ($320B+ FinTech market, $48B annual fraud losses), identifies key compliance deadlines (PCI-DSS 4.0: March 31, 2025; DORA: January 2025), and explains how the $1M transaction threshold triggers elevated compliance obligations.

**Content from B:**
> Applications processing high-value transactions face overlapping compliance obligations that must be addressed holistically. The intersection of PCI-DSS 4.0 and DORA creates a comprehensive governance structure spanning payment card data protection and broader operational resilience.
>
> The **$1 million USD** per-transaction threshold triggers several compliance escalations:
> - Elevated cardholder data environment (CDE) classification under PCI-DSS
> - Enhanced audit requirements under most financial regulatory frameworks
> - Mandatory transaction logging with extended retention periods

**Rationale:** Without regulatory context, technical checklists become disconnected from actual compliance requirements. Report B establishes why these controls matter at this transaction threshold.

---

## 1. OWASP Mobile Top 10 Compliance

Best source: **Report A (Single-pass)**

☐ Validate app signing certificate rotation procedures
☐ Confirm platform-appropriate biometric integration (LocalAuthentication framework / BiometricPrompt API)
```
